entries.thesis: Paylocity is the closest SMB-focused HCM pure-play peer; a cautious PAYX outlook on SMB hiring directly read-throughs to PCTY's bookings and seat growth.
Largest payroll processor with significant SMB exposure via RUN; same macro signal on employment checks/new business formation that drove PAYX caution applies, though ADP is more diversified.
Paycom shares the HCM software peer-group multiple and gets re-rated on SMB/mid-market payroll demand concerns even though it's slightly more upmarket.
QuickBooks Payroll and Mailchimp serve the same SMB cohort PAYX flagged as cautious; signals weaker SMB software spending into back half.
Soft SMB hiring environment flagged by PAYX implies slower contract-labor demand on Upwork, a marginal read-through.

Memory capex leverage — Lam has highest DRAM/NAND wafer fab equipment exposure (~30%+ memory). Bullish MU capex/HBM capacity commentary pulls forward WFE orders.
Micron is a key HBM3E supplier into Nvidia's Blackwell/Hopper GPUs; strong MU HBM commentary and bit-growth guide validates Nvidia data-center demand and supply ramp.
Significant memory WFE exposure; HBM requires advanced packaging/hybrid bonding tools where AMAT is positioned. Reads directly off MU capacity guide.
Test equipment small-cap with HBM/AI burn-in exposure; trades as a high-beta proxy on MU HBM commentary.
Custom ASIC/AI accelerator narrative leans on HBM availability; any MU commentary about HBM allocation tightening favoring Nvidia hurts merchant-silicon HBM access story.
Pure-play NAND peer post-SanDisk spin — re-rates on MU NAND pricing/bit guide as the cleanest US-listed read-through.
Process control intensity rises with HBM stacking yields; benefits from any MU capacity expansion commentary.
Cuts both ways — if MU signals NAND oversupply or pricing rollover, WDC as pure-play NAND peer gets hit hardest. Asymmetric downside if guide disappoints.
If MU flags HBM tightness or AI server demand softness, AI server builders dependent on memory-rich SKUs see margin/volume risk.

Large memory equipment exposure; memory capex digestion read-through hits AMAT's deposition/etch orders.
HBM-levered metrology play; if MU's HBM commentary disappointed, ONTO's AI-memory thesis takes a hit.
Pure-play NAND/HDD peer; Micron weak guide directly signals softer NAND pricing and demand into WDC's next quarter.
Memory is ~half of Lam's WFE revenue; a MU guide-down typically precedes memory capex cuts at MU/Samsung/SK Hynix.
